name: Advance America
description_short: Advance America La Puente, California — Advance America is the nation's largest payday lender with 800+ stores, offering payday, installment, and title ...
description_long:
Founded in 1997 and headquartered in Spartanburg, SC, Advance America grew to become the largest payday loan company in the United States by store count. The company went public on the NYSE in 2004 before being acquired in 2012 by Grupo Elektra, a Mexican financial services conglomerate, for approximately $780 million. Advance America operates more than 800 retail storefronts across 27 states and also offers online lending in select states. It carries no CDFI, HUD, or NFCC certifications — it is a for-profit, high-cost lender, not a nonprofit financial services provider. It holds an A+ rating from the Better Business Bureau and maintains BBB accreditation.

Advance America's core product is the payday loan — short-term cash advances of $100 to $1,800 due on the borrower's next payday, typically within two to four weeks. Beyond payday loans, the company offers installment loans ranging from $100 to $5,000 repaid over 3 to 36 months in fixed payments, and title loans of up to $25,000 secured by the borrower's vehicle. Lines of credit with revolving draw flexibility are also available in select states. In-store locations provide additional money services including check cashing and money transfers. Eligibility criteria are intentionally accessible — many loan types require no minimum credit score — making Advance America a last-resort option for borrowers locked out of conventional credit.

What distinguishes Advance America from smaller regional payday lenders is its national footprint and operational consistency. With more than two decades in business, the company has standardized its application process across channels — in-store, online, and mobile browser — and offers same-day funding at retail locations and next-business-day direct deposit for online applicants. Its scale also means standardized disclosures and state-compliant practices, which matters in a sector with historically poor consumer protection track records. The A+ BBB rating reflects a formal accreditation relationship, though this does not mitigate the structural cost of its loan products.

The fundamental limitation of Advance America is cost. APRs on payday loans routinely range from 200% to over 400%, and even its installment products carry APRs in the 200% range — for example, a $500 installment loan in Florida carries a 207.4% APR, and a $300 payday loan in Florida equates to a 304.17% APR. These rates are legal under applicable state laws but dramatically exceed what any bank, credit union, or even most online personal lenders charge. The company has been subject to CFPB examination and has accumulated consumer complaints over the years. Borrowers who cannot repay by the due date face rollover fees and debt cycles. Advance America is genuinely useful for a narrow use case — a true short-term cash emergency with a clear repayment path — but it is a costly choice by virtually any financial metric.
address: 1645 N Hacienda Blvd, La Puente, CA 91744

pricing.guarantee_details: No money-back guarantee. Loan terms are governed by state law and disclosed at origination.

tiers.name: Payday Loans
features: Repayment due on next payday (2–4 weeks)

tiers.name: Installment Loans
features: Example APR: 207.4% on $500 loan in Florida
Available in-store and online in select states

tiers.name: Title Loans
features: Vehicle remains in borrower's possession during loan term
Loan amount based on vehicle value

pros: Nation's largest payday lender with 800+ retail stores across 27 states
Same-day cash available in-store — no waiting for a bank transfer
No minimum credit score required on most loan products
Multiple loan types available: payday, installment, title loans, and lines of credit
A+ BBB rating with active accreditation
Apply in-store, online, or via mobile browser — three channels available
Nearly 30 years in business since 1997 — established and regulated lender
cons: APRs of 200%–500%+ are among the highest in consumer lending
Owned by Grupo Elektra, a Mexican conglomerate — not a U.S.-rooted company since 2012
Available in only 27 states — not an option for roughly half of U.S. consumers
History of CFPB scrutiny and consumer complaints on file
No credit-building benefit — payday loans are not designed to improve credit scores
best_for: Consumers with poor or no credit who need $100–$1,800 within 24 hours
Borrowers facing a one-time emergency expense who have a clear paycheck coming to repay the full balance
Unbanked or underbanked individuals needing in-person money services like check cashing or wire transfers
People in covered states who need a physical storefront rather than an online-only lender
diagnosis: Advance America is best suited for consumers who have exhausted every lower-cost option and need a small sum of cash immediately to cover an emergency, with a concrete plan to repay within weeks. The primary caveat is the cost: APRs of 200%–500% make this an extremely expensive form of credit that can trap borrowers in repeat-borrowing cycles if the initial loan cannot be repaid on schedule.
services: Online Account Portal (payment management, loan details)
Direct Deposit Funding (next business day for online loans)

cfpb_data.cfpb_company_name: Advance America, Cash Advance Centers, Inc.
